auth_config_env: SIGNOZ_TOKENIZER_JWT_SECRET (hardcoded 'secret' in default compose — JWT forgery surface)
default_creds: first registrant via POST /api/v1/register becomes admin (open until first claim)
misconfig_patterns: SIGNOZ_TOKENIZER_JWT_SECRET=secret hardcoded — JWT forge across any non-rotated deploy
Helm operators flip ClusterIP→LoadBalancer broadly, exposing 8123 ClickHouse
Docker quickstart maps 8080 + 4317 + 4318 directly to host

active_probe.false_positive_check: JSON shape with setup_completed boolean; setup_completed:false = open_registration_window (DO NOT POST register); setup_completed:true = invite_required

deployment_tells: ClickHouse 8123/9000 commented out in default compose (internal-only)
pivot_paths: /api/v1/version setup_completed:false → open admin claim window (NAMES are the finding, do not POST)
Shadow port 8123 ClickHouse HTTP → raw signoz_traces/signoz_logs/signoz_metrics databases
Shadow port 9000 ClickHouse native → same raw store, faster protocol
Hardcoded JWT secret 'secret' → forge admin token on non-rotated deploys
Shadow port 4317/4318 OTel ingest → trace poisoning surface
vulnerabilities: No published GHSAs as of 2026-06-09 (clean CVE field)
